title: Japanese-Style Deep Fried Shrimp
description: A crispy and flavorful appetizer or snack, perfect for any occasion. Simply marinate shrimp in a mixture of soy sauce and sake, then coat and fry to a golden brown.
instructions: In a shallow dish, mix together flour, a pinch of salt, and a pinch of pepper.
In a separate dish, beat the egg.
In another dish, mix together panko breadcrumbs and a pinch of salt.
Dredge the shrimp in the flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Dip the floured shrimp in the beaten egg, making sure they're fully coated.
Roll the egg-coated shrimp in the panko breadcrumb mixture, pressing the crumbs onto the shrimp to help them stick.
Heat about 1/2-inch (1 cm) of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (180°C).
When the oil is hot, add 3-4 shrimp to the skillet and fry for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and cooked through.
Remove the fried shrimp from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain any excess oil.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining shrimp.
Serve the deep-fried shrimp hot and enjoy!
